heading: 9.7.16.TensorCore 5th Generation Family Instructions (part 2)
content:
| Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
| --- | --- |
| A vector expression containing `.num` number of `.b32` registers as mentioned in the [Table 49](#tcgen05-num-shapes-ld). | r0, r1, … |

A warp executing `tcgen05{.ld,.st}.16x64b` will access 16 lanes of the Tensor Memory.
It loads from or stores to each of the lane (64 \* .num)-bits of data as shown in
[Figure 184](#tcgen05-mma-fragment-1664b).



[Figure: tcgen05-mma-fragment-1664b]
MATRIX FRAGMENT FOR SHAPE .16x64b

OVERVIEW:
This figure illustrates how a 16x64-bit matrix fragment is distributed across 32 lanes (threads) in a warp for NVIDIA PTX matrix operations. It shows the mapping between thread IDs (T0-T31), register names (r0, r1), and lane indices (0-31).

DIMENSIONS:
- Total width: 64 bits (indicated by arrow at top)
- Number of rows shown in grid: 16 (lanes 0-15 are populated with data)
- Lanes 16-31 are listed on the left but have no grid entries (empty rows)
- The grid has 4 columns of data cells

LANE NUMBERING:
Left side label: "lane % 32"
Lanes 0 through 31 are listed vertically on the left side.
Only lanes 0-15 contain data in the grid; lanes 16-31 are empty.

GRID STRUCTURE (4 columns x 16 rows):
The grid is divided into two halves horizontally:
- Left half (columns 1-2): corresponds to span .x1
- Full width (columns 1-4): corresponds to span .x2

The "..." symbol appears to the right of the grid, indicating the pattern continues.

CELL CONTENTS (format: ThreadID : RegisterName):

Lane 0:  T0:r0  | T2:r0  | T0:r1  | T2:r1
Lane 1:  T4:r0  | T6:r0  | T4:r1  | T6:r1
Lane 2:  T8:r0  | T10:r0 | T8:r1  | T10:r1
Lane 3:  T12:r0 | T14:r0 | T12:r1 | T14:r1
Lane 4:  T16:r0 | T18:r0 | T16:r1 | T18:r1
Lane 5:  T20:r0 | T22:r0 | T20:r1 | T22:r1
Lane 6:  T24:r0 | T26:r0 | T24:r1 | T26:r1
Lane 7:  T28:r0 | T30:r0 | T28:r1 | T30:r1
Lane 8:  T1:r0  | T3:r0  | T1:r1  | T3:r1
Lane 9:  T5:r0  | T7:r0  | T5:r1  | T7:r1
Lane 10: T9:r0  | T11:r0 | T9:r1  | T11:r1
Lane 11: T13:r0 | T15:r0 | T13:r1 | T15:r1
Lane 12: T17:r0 | T19:r0 | T17:r1 | T19:r1
Lane 13: T21:r0 | T23:r0 | T21:r1 | T23:r1
Lane 14: T25:r0 | T27:r0 | T25:r1 | T27:r1
Lane 15: T29:r0 | T31:r0 | T29:r1 | T31:r1

REGISTER SPANS (arrows below the grid):
- .x1: spans columns 1-2 (left half, approximately 32 bits)
- .x2: spans columns 1-4 (full width, 64 bits)

THREAD DISTRIBUTION PATTERN:
- Lanes 0-7 hold even-numbered threads: T0, T2, T4, T6, T8, T10, T12, T14, T16, T18, T20, T22, T24, T26, T28, T30
- Lanes 8-15 hold odd-numbered threads: T1, T3, T5, T7, T9, T11, T13, T15, T17, T19, T21, T23, T25, T27, T29, T31
- Each thread contributes two registers: r0 (left two columns) and r1 (right two columns)
- Each thread appears twice per row (in two adjacent columns within each half)
- Lanes 16-31 are empty, indicating this fragment only uses 16 of the 32 lanes

COLOR CODING:
Cells are color-coded by thread group, with distinct colors used to visually group related threads (e.g., T0/T2 share a color, T4/T6 share a color, etc.), cycling through approximately 8 distinct colors across the 16 lanes.


Figure 184 Matrix Fragment for shape .16x64b

###### 9.7.16.2.3.1.3. [Matrix fragments for shape .16x128b](#tcgen05-matrix-fragments-shape-16128b)

A `tcgen05{.ld,.st}.16x128b` instruction has the following data vector register.

| Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
| --- | --- |
| A vector expression containing `.num` number of `.b32` registers as mentioned in the [Table 49](#tcgen05-num-shapes-ld). | r0, r1, … |

A warp executing `tcgen05{.ld,.st}.16x128b` will access 16 lanes of the Tensor Memory.
It loads from or stores to each of the lane (128 \* .num)-bits of data as shown in
[Figure 185](#tcgen05-mma-fragment-16128b).



[Figure: tcgen05-mma-fragment-16128b]
MATRIX FRAGMENT FOR SHAPE .16x128b

OVERVIEW:
This figure illustrates the data layout of a matrix fragment with shape .16x128b as used in NVIDIA PTX ISA warp-level matrix operations. It shows how matrix data is distributed across the 32 lanes of a warp, with each cell indicating which thread (T) and which register (r) holds that particular piece of data.

DIMENSIONS AND STRUCTURE:
- Total width: 128 bits (indicated by a double-headed arrow spanning the full width at the top)
- The grid has 16 rows (rows 0 through 15) and 4 columns of cells
- Rows 16 through 31 are listed on the left side but are empty (no data), indicating those lanes hold no data for this fragment
- A label ".x1" with a double-headed arrow spans the full width at row 16, indicating this is the x1 repetition or tile dimension

LANE INDEXING:
- Left side label: "lane % 32"
- Lane indices 0 through 31 are listed vertically on the left
- Only lanes 0 through 15 (rows 0-15) contain data
- Lanes 16 through 31 are empty

CELL CONTENTS FORMAT:
Each cell contains a label of the form "Tn : rm" where:
- Tn = Thread number n (the warp lane that owns this data element)
- rm = Register index m (r0 or r1)

COMPLETE CELL-BY-CELL DATA (row : col0, col1, col2, col3):

Row 0:  T0:r0,   T1:r0,   T2:r0,   T3:r0
Row 1:  T4:r0,   T5:r0,   T6:r0,   T7:r0
Row 2:  T8:r0,   T9:r0,   T10:r0,  T11:r0
Row 3:  T12:r0,  T13:r0,  T14:r0,  T15:r0
Row 4:  T16:r0,  T17:r0,  T18:r0,  T19:r0
Row 5:  T20:r0,  T21:r0,  T22:r0,  T23:r0
Row 6:  T24:r0,  T25:r0,  T26:r0,  T27:r0
Row 7:  T28:r0,  T29:r0,  T30:r0,  T31:r0
Row 8:  T0:r1,   T1:r1,   T2:r1,   T3:r1
Row 9:  T4:r1,   T5:r1,   T6:r1,   T7:r1
Row 10: T8:r1,   T9:r1,   T10:r1,  T11:r1
Row 11: T12:r1,  T13:r1,  T14:r1,  T15:r1
Row 12: T16:r1,  T17:r1,  T18:r1,  T19:r1
Row 13: T20:r1,  T21:r1,  T22:r1,  T23:r1
Row 14: T24:r1,  T25:r1,  T26:r1,  T27:r1
Row 15: T28:r1,  T29:r1,  T30:r1,  T31:r1

COLOR CODING:
The cells use alternating colors to visually distinguish groups of threads. The color pattern repeats in groups of 4 threads across rows:
- Column 0 (T0,T4,T8,...): light green/teal tones
- Column 1 (T1,T5,T9,...): light yellow/tan tones
- Column 2 (T2,T6,T10,...): light orange/salmon tones
- Column 3 (T3,T7,T11,...): darker orange/brown tones
Colors repeat consistently for r0 (rows 0-7) and r1 (rows 8-15).

An ellipsis "..." appears to the right of row 7, suggesting additional repetitions or tiles may exist beyond what is shown.

ORGANIZATION PATTERN:
- All 32 threads (T0-T31) contribute to this fragment
- Each thread contributes 2 registers: r0 and r1
- Rows 0-7 use register r0 for all threads T0-T31 (4 threads per row, 8 rows = 32 threads)
- Rows 8-15 use register r1 for all threads T0-T31 (same layout repeated)
- The fragment spans 128 bits wide and 16 rows tall
- The .x1 annotation indicates this represents one horizontal tile of the fragment


Figure 185 Matrix Fragment for shape .16x128b

###### 9.7.16.2.3.1.4. [Matrix fragments for shape .16x256b](#tcgen05-matrix-fragments-shape-16256b)

A `tcgen05{.ld,.st}.16x256b` instruction has the following data vector register.

| Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
| --- | --- |
| A vector expression containing `.num` number of `.b32` registers as mentioned in the [Table 49](#tcgen05-num-shapes-ld). | r0, r1, r2, r3, … |

A warp executing `tcgen05{.ld,.st}.16x256b` will access 16 lanes of the Tensor Memory.
It loads from or stores to each of the lane (256 \* .num)-bits of data as shown in
[Figure 186](#tcgen05-mma-fragment-16256b).



[Figure: tcgen05-mma-fragment-16256b]
MATRIX FRAGMENT FOR SHAPE .16x256b

OVERVIEW:
This figure illustrates how a 16x256-bit matrix fragment is distributed across threads (lanes) in a warp for NVIDIA PTX matrix operations. It shows the mapping between warp lanes (0-31) and thread registers, indicating which thread holds which register fragment of the matrix.

DIMENSIONS AND LAYOUT:
- The matrix is 256 bits wide (indicated by a double-headed arrow labeled "256-bits" spanning the full width at the top)
- The matrix is 16 rows tall (rows 0 through 15 are populated; rows 16 through 31 are shown but empty)
- The vertical axis is labeled "lane % 32" on the left side
- Lane numbers run from 0 to 31 on the left
- Only lanes 0-15 contain data in the visible grid; lanes 16-31 are empty
- A double-headed arrow at the bottom spanning the full width is labeled ".x1" indicating this is one repetition

GRID STRUCTURE:
The populated grid (lanes 0-15) contains 8 columns of cells, each cell containing a label of the form "TX : rY" where X is a thread number and Y is a register index.

COLOR CODING (column groups, left to right):
- Columns 1-2: Light gray/white background
- Columns 3-4: Light green background
- Columns 5-6: Light yellow/gold background
- Columns 7-8: Dark teal/green background (rightmost two columns)
- Note: The "..." symbol appears to the right of the last column, indicating the pattern continues

CELL CONTENTS BY ROW (lane):

Lane 0:  T0:r0,  T0:r1,  T1:r0,  T1:r1,  T2:r0,  T2:r1,  T3:r0,  T3:r1
Lane 1:  T4:r0,  T4:r1,  T5:r0,  T5:r1,  T6:r0,  T6:r1,  T7:r0,  T7:r1
Lane 2:  T8:r0,  T8:r1,  T9:r0,  T9:r1,  T10:r0, T10:r1, T11:r0, T11:r1
Lane 3:  T12:r0, T12:r1, T13:r0, T13:r1, T14:r0, T14:r1, T15:r0, T15:r1
Lane 4:  T16:r0, T16:r1, T17:r0, T17:r1, T18:r0, T18:r1, T19:r0, T19:r1
Lane 5:  T20:r0, T20:r1, T21:r0, T21:r1, T22:r0, T22:r1, T23:r0, T23:r1
Lane 6:  T24:r0, T24:r1, T25:r0, T25:r1, T26:r0, T26:r1, T27:r0, T27:r1
Lane 7:  T28:r0, T28:r1, T29:r0, T29:r1, T30:r0, T30:r1, T31:r0, T31:r1
Lane 8:  T0:r2,  T0:r3,  T1:r2,  T1:r3,  T2:r2,  T2:r3,  T3:r2,  T3:r3
Lane 9:  T4:r2,  T4:r3,  T5:r2,  T5:r3,  T6:r2,  T6:r3,  T7:r2,  T7:r3
Lane 10: T8:r2,  T8:r3,  T9:r2,  T9:r3,  T10:r2, T10:r3, T11:r2, T11:r3
Lane 11: T12:r2, T12:r3, T13:r2, T13:r3, T14:r2, T14:r3, T15:r2, T15:r3
Lane 12: T16:r2, T16:r3, T17:r2, T17:r3, T18:r2, T18:r3, T19:r2, T19:r3
Lane 13: T20:r2, T20:r3, T21:r2, T21:r3, T22:r2, T22:r3, T23:r2, T23:r3
Lane 14: T24:r2, T24:r3, T25:r2, T25:r3, T26:r2, T26:r3, T27:r2, T27:r3
Lane 15: T28:r2, T28:r3, T29:r2, T29:r3, T30:r2, T30:r3, T31:r2, T31:r3

Lanes 16-31: Empty (no data assigned)

KEY PATTERNS AND OBSERVATIONS:

Thread distribution pattern:
- Lanes 0-7 hold registers r0 and r1 for threads T0-T31
- Lanes 8-15 hold registers r2 and r3 for threads T0-T31
- Each group of 8 consecutive lanes covers all 32 threads (T0-T31)
- Within each lane, two consecutive register values (e.g., r0 and r1) from the same thread appear in adjacent columns

Thread numbering pattern across columns:
- Each pair of columns corresponds to one thread
- Column pair 1-2: T0, T4, T8, T12, T16, T20, T24, T28 (for lanes 0-7) and same threads for lanes 8-15
- Column pair 3-4: T1, T5, T9, T13, T17, T21, T25, T29
- Column pair 5-6: T2, T6, T10, T14, T18, T22, T26, T30
- Column pair 7-8: T3, T7, T11, T15, T19, T23, T27, T31
- Threads increment by 4 going down each column within a group of 8 lanes

Register assignment:
- Each thread contributes 4 registers total (r0, r1, r2, r3)
- r0 and r1 appear in lanes 0-7
- r2 and r3 appear in lanes 8-15
- The ".x1" label indicates this fragment occupies one 256-bit segment (no repetition beyond what is shown)


Figure 186 Matrix Fragment for shape .16x256b

###### 9.7.16.2.3.1.5. [Matrix fragments for shape .16x32bx2](#tcgen05-matrix-fragments-shape-1632b2)

A `tcgen05{.ld,.st}.16x32bx2` instruction has the following data vector register.

| Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
| --- | --- |
| A vector expression containing `.num` number of `.b32` registers as mentioned in the [Table 49](#tcgen05-num-shapes-ld). | r0, r1, … |

A warp executing `tcgen05{.ld,.st}.16x32bx2` will access 16 lanes of the Tensor Memory.
It loads from or stores to each of the lane (32 \* .num)-bits of data as shown in
[Figure 187](#tcgen05-mma-fragment-1632b2).



[Figure: tcgen05-mma-fragment-1632b2]
Matrix Fragment for shape .16x32bx2

CONCEPT OVERVIEW:
This figure illustrates the memory layout and thread register mapping for a matrix fragment with shape .16x32bx2 in NVIDIA PTX ISA. It shows how a 32-lane warp accesses matrix data split across two memory regions, each 32 bits wide, with threads mapped to specific register slots.

OVERALL STRUCTURE:
The figure shows two vertical columns of register assignments, one for lanes 0-15 (left column) and one for lanes 16-31 (right column). The left column is positioned at the Base address, and the right column is positioned at Base address + immHalfSplitOffset. Both columns are 32 bits wide.

LEFT COLUMN (lanes 0 through 15):
- Labeled at top: "32-bits" (horizontal arrow spanning the column width)
- Labeled on left: "lane % 32" indicating the row index is the lane number modulo 32
- Arrow on left side at row 17 area labeled ".x1" indicating register index
- Rows and their thread/register assignments (each cell is color-coded):
  Lane 0:  T0 : r0   (light green)
  Lane 1:  T1 : r0   (light blue)
  Lane 2:  T2 : r0   (light orange/yellow)
  Lane 3:  T3 : r0   (light purple/lavender)
  Lane 4:  T4 : r0   (light salmon/pink)
  Lane 5:  T5 : r0   (light teal/cyan)
  Lane 6:  T6 : r0   (light gray)
  Lane 7:  T7 : r0   (dark gray/charcoal)
  Lane 8:  T8 : r0   (light green, slightly different shade)
  Lane 9:  T9 : r0   (light blue, slightly different shade)
  Lane 10: T10 : r0  (light orange/yellow)
  Lane 11: T11 : r0  (light purple)
  Lane 12: T12 : r0  (light pink/salmon)
  Lane 13: T13 : r0  (light teal)
  Lane 14: T14 : r0  (light gray)
  Lane 15: T15 : r0  (dark gray/charcoal)
- A "..." ellipsis appears to the right of lane 9, indicating additional columns not shown
- Lanes 16-31 in the left column are EMPTY (no register assignments shown)

RIGHT COLUMN (lanes 16 through 31):
- Labeled at top: "32-bits" (horizontal arrow spanning the column width)
- Arrow labeled "Base address" points to the top of the left column
- Arrow labeled "Base address + immHalfSplitOffset" points to the top of the right column
- Arrow on left side at bottom labeled ".x1" indicating register index
- Rows and their thread/register assignments:
  Lane 16: T16 : r0  (light green)
  Lane 17: T17 : r0  (light blue)
  Lane 18: T18 : r0  (light orange/yellow)
  Lane 19: T19 : r0  (light purple/lavender)
  Lane 20: T20 : r0  (light salmon/pink)
  Lane 21: T21 : r0  (light teal/cyan)
  Lane 22: T22 : r0  (light gray)
  Lane 23: T23 : r0  (dark gray/charcoal)
  Lane 24: T24 : r0  (light green)
  Lane 25: T25 : r0  (light blue)
  Lane 26: T26 : r0  (orange/yellow)
  Lane 27: T27 : r0  (light purple)
  Lane 28: T28 : r0  (dark red/brown)
  Lane 29: T29 : r0  (dark navy/black)
  Lane 30: T30 : r0  (dark gray)
  Lane 31: T31 : r0  (black)
- A "..." ellipsis appears to the right of lane 23, indicating additional columns not shown

KEY ANNOTATIONS:
- "lane % 32": The vertical axis index represents the lane number modulo 32
- "32-bits": Each column (memory region) is 32 bits wide
- "Base address": The starting memory address for the first half (lanes 0-15)
- "Base address + immHalfSplitOffset": The starting memory address for the second half (lanes 16-31), offset by an immediate half-split offset value
- ".x1": Appears twice (once per column on the left side), indicating the register file index or stride dimension is 1

DATA ORGANIZATION PATTERN:
- All 32 threads (T0 through T31) each hold register r0
- The warp is split into two halves: lanes 0-15 access data at the Base address, and lanes 16-31 access data at Base address + immHalfSplitOffset
- Each thread contributes one 32-bit register (r0) to the matrix fragment
- The "2" in .16x32bx2 refers to this two-region split (two halves of the warp accessing two separate memory locations)
- The color coding of cells repeats in a pattern of 16 colors, cycling through both halves, suggesting a correspondence between lane 0 and lane 16, lane 1 and lane 17, etc.
- The "..." ellipsis in both columns indicates that additional register columns (beyond r0) exist but are not shown in this figure


Figure 187 Matrix Fragment for shape .16x32bx2

#### 9.7.16.3. [Major-ness supported by Strides](#tcgen05-majorness-supported-by-strides)

There are two strides involved while accessing a matrix from shared memory:

1. Leading dimension stride (byte offset or absolute address)
2. Stride dimension byte offset

##### 9.7.16.3.1. [Leading Dimension Stride: relative offset or absolute address](#tcgen05-leading-dimension-byte-offset)

There are two modes of Leading Dimension Strides as described below.
Bit #52 in the [Shared memory descriptor](#tcgen05-shared-memory-descriptor) is used to distinguish between two modes.

###### 9.7.16.3.1.1. [Relative offset mode](#tcgen05-leading-dimension-byte-offset-relative-offset)

In this mode, the leading dimension stride is specified as a relative byte offset between the
columns as explained in the below table.
The leading dimension stride can either be specified as a relative offset between the columns
or as an absolute byte address of next buffer. The leading dimension stride is defined
differently for transposed and non-transposed matrices. The leading dimension stride is defined
as follows for matrices whose element types are normalized to 128-bits:

| Major-ness | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| K-Major | * No-Swizzling: the stride from the first column to the second column   of the 8x2 tile in the 128-bit element type normalized matrix. * Swizzled layouts: not used, assumed to be 1. |
| MN-Major | * Interleave: stride from the first 8 columns to the next 8 columns. * Swizzled layouts: stride from the first (swizzle-byte-size/16) rows   to the next (swizzle-byte-size/16) rows. |

###### 9.7.16.3.1.2. [Absolute address mode for K dimension being 48B](#tcgen05-leading-dimension-byte-offset-absolute-address)

The `tcgen05.mma` instruction with *K-dimension* of 48B would overflow the 128B
shared memory boundary if the data is packed contiguously.

In this case, the absolute address mode can be used to break up the data in the
shared memory into two chunks such that both these chunks are laid out within
the aligned 128-byte address boundary.
The leading dimension absolute address can point to the second data chunk in the shared memory.

###### 9.7.16.3.1.2.1. [Restrictions on the Leading Dimension Absolute Address Stride](#tcgen05-leading-dimension-byte-offset-absolute-address-restriction)

Following are the restrictions on the absolute address stride mode:

1. Only 128B swizzle (with 16B atomicity) is supported.
2. Only K-Major mode is supported. That is, the transpose bits(bits #15 and #16) in
   [Instruction descriptor](#tcgen05-instruction-descriptor) must be 0.
3. The matrix base offset must be 0.

##### 9.7.16.3.2. [Stride Dimension Byte Offset](#tcgen05-stride-dimension-byte-offset)

The stride dimension byte offset is defined differently for transposed and non-transposed
matrices. The stride dimension byte offset is defined as follows for matrices whose element
types are normalized to 128-bits:

| Major-ness | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| K-Major | The offset from the first 8 rows to the next 8 rows. |
| MN-Major | * Interleave: offset from the first row to the next row. * Swizzled layout: offset from the first 8 columns to the next 8   columns |

##### 9.7.16.3.3. [Canonical Layouts](#tcgen05-canonical-layouts)

In terms of [CuTe layouts](https://docs.nvidia.com/cutlass/media/docs/cpp/cute/01_layout.html)
the canonical layout can be expressed as follows:

| Major- ness | Swizzling mode | Canonical Layout without swizzling | [Swizzling](https://github.com/NVIDIA/cutlass/blob/bf9da7b76c766d7ee7d536afc77880a4ef1f1156/include/cute/swizzle.hpp) on the previous column |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MN- major | No-swizzling or Interleaved | ((T,1,m),(8,k)):((1,T,SBO),(1T,LBO)) | Swizzle<0, 4, 3> |
| 32B Swizzling | ((T,2,m),(8,k)):((1,T,LBO),(2T,SBO)) | Swizzle<1, 4, 3> |
| 64B Swizzling | ((T,4,m),(8,k)):((1,T,LBO),(4T,SBO)) | Swizzle<2, 4, 3> |
| 128B Swizzling | ((T,8,m),(8,k)):((1,T,LBO),(8T,SBO)) | Swizzle<3, 4, 3> |
| K- major | No-swizzling or Interleaved | ((8,m),(T,2k)):((1T,SBO),(1,LBO)) | Swizzle<0, 4, 3> |
| 32B Swizzling | ((8,m),(T,2k)):((2T,SBO),(1,T)) | Swizzle<1, 4, 3> |
| 64B Swizzling | ((8,m),(T,2k)):((4T,SBO),(1,T)) | Swizzle<2, 4, 3> |
| 128B Swizzling | ((8,m),(T,2k)):((8T,SBO),(1,T)) | Swizzle<3, 4, 3> |

where

* T = 128 / sizeof-elements-in-bits
  T represents scale factor which normalizes matrix element types to 128-bits.
* m represents the number of repeating patterns across rows.
* k represents the number of repeating patterns across columns.

Examples

* K-Major, no-swizzling and tf32 type: [Figure 188](#tcgen05-k-no-swizzle-tf32)

  

[Figure: async-warpgroup-k-no-swizzle-tf32]
CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:
This figure illustrates the memory layout mapping for a shared memory matrix tile using K-major ordering with no swizzling and tf32 (tensor float 32) data type. It shows how 256 elements (numbered 0 through 255) are arranged in a 16x16 grid, indicating which thread or memory location accesses which element index. This is relevant to the ldmatrix or wgmma (warp group matrix multiply-accumulate) instructions in PTX ISA where shared memory layout determines data access patterns.

GRID DIMENSIONS:
The grid is 16 columns wide (labeled 0 through 15 along the top) and 16 rows tall (labeled 0 through 15 along the left side). This forms a 16x16 = 256 element matrix.

ELEMENT NUMBERING PATTERN:
Each cell contains a unique integer from 0 to 255. The numbering does NOT follow a simple row-major sequential order across the full grid. Instead, the pattern reflects K-major (column-major in the K dimension) ordering with a specific tiling structure.

The values in each row are as follows:

Row 0:  0,   1,   2,   3,   64,  65,  66,  67,  128, 129, 130, 131, 192, 193, 194, 195
Row 1:  4,   5,   6,   7,   68,  69,  70,  71,  132, 133, 134, 135, 196, 197, 198, 199
Row 2:  8,   9,   10,  11,  72,  73,  74,  75,  136, 137, 138, 139, 200, 201, 202, 203
Row 3:  12,  13,  14,  15,  76,  77,  78,  79,  140, 141, 142, 143, 204, 205, 206, 207

TILING STRUCTURE AND PATTERN ANALYSIS:
The 16x16 grid is divided into four horizontal bands of 4 columns each (columns 0-3, columns 4-7, columns 8-11, columns 12-15). Within each band:
- Columns 0-3: elements 0-63, sequential in groups of 4 per row, incrementing by 4 per row downward (0,1,2,3 in row 0; 4,5,6,7 in row 1; ... 60,61,62,63 in row 15)
- Columns 4-7: elements 64-127, same pattern offset by 64
- Columns 8-11: elements 128-191, same pattern offset by 128
- Columns 12-15: elements 192-255, same pattern offset by 192

Within each 4-column band, elements increment by 1 across the 4 columns and by 4 going down each row. This means the K-major layout stores consecutive K-dimension elements contiguously in groups of 4, then proceeds down the row (M) dimension.

COLOR CODING:
The rows are color-coded in a repeating 4-row cycle to visually group sets of 4 rows together:
- Rows 0, 4, 8, 12: Blue/purple background
- Rows 1, 5, 9, 13: Green background
- Rows 2, 6, 10, 14: Yellow/light yellow background
- Rows 3, 7, 11, 15: Pink/salmon/red background

This 4-row color grouping repeats four times across the 16 rows, likely indicating warp-level or thread-group-level data partitioning.

AXIS LABELS:
- Top axis (columns): labeled 0 through 15 from left to right
- Left axis (rows): labeled 0 through 15 from top to bottom
- No additional axis titles are shown in the figure itself; the caption provides the context: K major, no-swizzling, tf32 type

SUMMARY OF DATA ORGANIZATION:
The layout divides the 16x16 tile into four 16x4 vertical slabs. Each slab holds 64 consecutive elements. Within each slab, elements are stored in row-major order with 4 elements per row. The K-major designation means the K (inner/reduction) dimension varies fastest within each group of 4 consecutive elements. The no-swizzling designation means there is no XOR-based address permutation applied to avoid bank conflicts. The tf32 type means each element is a 32-bit tensor float value, so each element occupies 4 bytes in memory.


  Figure 188 K major, no-swizzling and tf32 type

  the strides and related details are as follows:

  Exact layout : Swizzle<0,4,3> o ((8,2),(4,4)):((4,32),(1,64))

  Canonical Layout :Swizzle<0,4,3> o ((8,m),(T,2k)):((1T,SBO),(1,LBO))

  | Parameters | Value |
  | --- | --- |
  | T | 4 |
  | m | 2 |
  | k | 2 |
  | LBO (relative offset) | 64\*sizeof(tf32) |
  | SBO | 32\*sizeof(tf32) |
  | Encoding of LBO in descriptor | (LBO) >> 4 = 16 |
  | Encoding of SBO in descriptor | (SBO) >> 4 = 8 |
* K-Major, 32B swizzling and tf32 type: [Figure 189](#tcgen05-k-32b-swizzle-tf32)

  

[Figure: async-warpgroup-k-32B-swizzle-tf32]
This figure illustrates the memory layout and swizzling pattern for a K-major matrix with 32-byte swizzling and tf32 data type, as used in NVIDIA PTX ISA wgmma (warp group matrix multiply-accumulate) or similar tensor core operations.

OVERALL STRUCTURE:
The figure is a 16x16 grid of cells. The rows are labeled 0 through 15 on the left side (vertical axis). The columns are labeled 0 through 15 on the top (horizontal axis). Each cell contains a numerical value representing a memory element index (i.e., the logical-to-physical address mapping after swizzling is applied).

COLOR CODING (indicating swizzle groups or bank assignments):
The cells are colored in a repeating pattern of four colors across groups of rows, cycling every 4 rows:
- Purple/blue: rows 0, 4, 8, 12 (and specific column groupings within)
- Green: rows 1, 5, 9, 13
- Yellow: rows 2, 6, 10, 14
- Pink/red: rows 3, 7, 11, 15

Within each row, the coloring also shifts based on column groupings, reflecting the swizzle pattern. Specifically, within each row the color of cells shifts in groups of 4 columns, creating a diagonal swizzle pattern across the grid.

SWIZZLE PATTERN ANALYSIS:
The tf32 type is 4 bytes per element. With 32-byte swizzling, groups of 8 tf32 elements (8 x 4 = 32 bytes) form a swizzle unit.

The column axis (0-15) represents the K dimension (K-major layout). The row axis (0-15) represents the other matrix dimension.

Key observations about the swizzle:
- Rows 0-3 (first group of 4): columns 0-7 hold indices 0-31 sequentially (no swap), columns 8-15 hold indices 128-159 sequentially.
- Rows 4-7 (second group of 4): columns 0-3 and 4-7 are swapped relative to sequential order (e.g., row 4 has 36,37,38,39 in cols 0-3 and 32,33,34,35 in cols 4-7), and similarly for the right half.
- Rows 8-11 (third group of 4): same pattern as rows 0-3 (no swap within the half-row groups).
- Rows 12-15 (fourth group of 4): same swap pattern as rows 4-7.

The swizzle effectively XORs the column group index with a function of the row index to avoid shared memory bank conflicts when loading tf32 data in K-major order. The left half (columns 0-7) contains indices in the range 0-127 and the right half (columns 8-15) contains indices in the range 128-255, with the swizzle applied within each half independently.


  Figure 189 K major, 32B swizzling and tf32 type

  the strides and related details are as follows:

  Exact layout : Swizzle<1,4,3> o ((8,2),(4,4)):((8,64),(1,4))

  Canonical Layout :Swizzle<1,4,3> o ((8,m),(T,2k)):((2T,SBO),(1,T))
